data.narrative:
Parry Labs operates in Defense & Security at the Series B stage. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms. The private peer set includes Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, all at Series B stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $80M in total capital raised as a proxy base value, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $152M. Peer-median analysis draws from six same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B (median: $1.2B), reflecting the valuation density in late-stage defense tech.

The two methods produce a wide implied range: $152M (stage-progression low end) to $1.2B (peer median). Triangulating across both approaches yields a central estimate of $676M with a full range of $129M to $1.4B. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects material uncertainty around whether Parry Labs' fundraising velocity and operational metrics align with the higher-capitalized peer cohort. Key sensitivities include the company's revenue run-rate relative to peer benchmarks and the intensity of defense-sector demand pull on comparable valuations.
